skill.name: angular-cdk-integration
skill.description: Guides creation of Angular components with CDK drag-drop, overlay, portal, virtual scrolling, accessibility, clipboard, and platform utilities.
skill.summary: Build Angular CDK user interface components with practical standalone component patterns.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found the flagged content is documentation and TypeScript examples, not executable skill code. No prompt injection, command execution, credential access, or data exfiltration intent was found; residual risk is limited to normal use of documented Angular CDK examples and external documentation links.

low_findings.title: Markdown and Template Literal False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and Angular TypeScript template literals. The file provides examples for CDK components and does not instruct the agent to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced code blocks, component templates, or string interpolation examples. No Ruby code, shell execution, or command invocation appears in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are keyword matches inside ordinary documentation text, such as description fields and cleanup guidance. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher selection, or credential handling appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found no cryptography implementation or security-sensitive algorithm choice. The matches are incidental text in Angular CDK documentation examples.

low_findings.title: Platform Detection Documentation Is Benign
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings refer to Angular CDK platform detection examples that show browser and mobile capability flags. They do not execute system discovery commands, access files, read environment variables, or transmit collected data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious locations are Angular component methods, CSS borders, and platform flags. The intent is responsive UI behavior, not host reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example Values
low_findings.description: The URL findings are a sample application share link and official Angular documentation references. No code in the skill makes network requests or sends user data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a clipboard example and reference list only. I found no fetch, HTTP client call, webhook, telemetry, or exfiltration behavior.

content.value_statement: Angular CDK features can be difficult to wire correctly in standalone components. This skill gives focused patterns for drag-drop, overlays, virtual scrolling, clipboard, platform checks, and accessibility.
actual_capabilities: Shows standalone Angular component patterns for CDK drag and drop.
Demonstrates overlay creation with positioning, backdrop handling, and disposal.
Provides a virtual scrolling example for large task lists.
Explains clipboard copy behavior with success feedback.
Shows Angular CDK platform checks for browser and mobile behavior.
Includes integration guidance for accessibility, cleanup, and UI-layer boundaries.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install Angular CDK packages.
Examples are tailored to an ng-events style project and may need adaptation.
It does not provide automated tests or migration scripts.
It does not cover every Angular CDK module.

use_cases.title: Create a Task Board
use_cases.description: Use CDK drag-drop patterns to build sortable task columns with connected drop lists.

use_cases.title: Add Floating Menus
use_cases.description: Use CDK overlay and portal guidance to create positioned menus, popovers, or tooltips.

use_cases.title: Improve Large Lists
use_cases.description: Use virtual scrolling guidance to render high-volume task or blueprint lists more efficiently.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Drag-Drop Component
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a simple Angular CDK board.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standalone Angular component that uses Angular CDK drag-drop for three task columns. Include required imports and cleanup notes.

prompt_templates.title: Overlay Menu Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a floating action menu.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Angular CDK overlay menu for a button. Include positioning, backdrop behavior, disposal, and accessibility considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Virtual Scroll Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: A list renders too many rows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor an Angular task list to use CDK virtual scrolling. Explain the template changes, sizing assumptions, and testing checks.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced CDK Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You need architecture guidance before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Angular feature plan for CDK usage. Identify where drag-drop, overlay, clipboard, platform detection, and accessibility utilities should be used.

output_examples.input: Build a drag-drop task board with Angular CDK.
output_examples.output: A component plan with standalone imports, connected task columns, drop handling, and notes for touch interaction testing.

output_examples.input: Add a menu that opens beside a button.
output_examples.output: An overlay implementation outline with position strategy, backdrop handling, portal attachment, and disposal requirements.

output_examples.input: Make a large list faster in Angular.
output_examples.output: A virtual scrolling approach with viewport sizing, row height guidance, and checks for accessibility and performance.

best_practices: Import only the Angular CDK modules needed by the component.
Dispose overlays and subscriptions when components close or are destroyed.
Test drag-drop, overlays, and virtual lists with keyboard and screen reader workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not place CDK UI behavior inside repositories or domain services.
Do not create overlays without backdrop or disposal handling.
Do not use virtual scrolling when item heights are unknown without planning layout constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill install Angular CDK?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples. The project must already include the needed Angular CDK packages.

faq.question: Is it only for drag and drop?
faq.answer: No. It also covers overlays, portals, virtual scrolling, clipboard actions, platform detection, and accessibility concerns.

faq.question: Can I use it with standalone Angular components?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use standalone component patterns and direct CDK imports.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It reminds users to test focus behavior, keyboard workflows, screen readers, and mobile interactions.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No. The skill file contains documentation links and a sample URL, but it does not perform network operations.

faq.question: What Angular version does it target?
faq.answer: The skill states compatibility with Angular CDK 20.x and Angular 20.3.x.
